name: Particle Swarm Optimization
key_facts: Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is a population-based stochastic optimization technique inspired by social behavior of bird flocking
Each particle maintains a position and velocity in the search space
Particles are influenced by their personal best position and the global best position found by the swarm
Key parameters include inertia weight, cognitive coefficient, and social coefficient
PSO was introduced by Kennedy and Eberhart in 1995
PSO is derivative-free and works well for continuous optimization problems
Velocity update combines inertia, personal experience, and social influence
forbidden_claims: PSO guarantees convergence to the global optimum
PSO uses crossover and mutation operators like genetic algorithms
PSO was inspired by ant colony behavior
